So we are, I guess, we're in the last mile delivery space, parcel delivery specifically, but we're a B2B shipper.

So we work with big e-commerce companies like Macy's, Saks, Lululemon, Adidas, like any big either hybrid brick and mortar e-commerce company that's shipping directly to consumers, we fulfill for them.
So we have specific warehouses across the country, and then we have partnerships with third-party drivers, so similar to a DoorDash or Uber Eats or whatever.
Those folks that are independent contractors can come in, pick up packages, and actually deliver on our behalf.
So they don't work for Veho, but they are similar to the same kind of partnership like an Instacart or a DoorDash driver would have.
So it's sort of our differentiation.
We're able to help deliver for these companies at a lower cost because we're super asset light, if that makes sense.
We don't have trucks.
We don't work with unions in the same way that like UPS or...
We work
FedEx do.

And so we're able to like flex up and down with demand for these companies during peak season, like right now, Black Friday, Christmas, they need, you know, a lot more drivers on the road because demand is higher.
And so we're able to flex up and down and sort of meet them where they're at and create these sort of like bespoke packages for them that actually ends up like saving them a ton of money.
But we consider ourselves a technology company.
We have quite a few like proprietary pieces of technology and then also specific apps for the consumer.
for the driver, for the entire flywheel.
And we sort of operate on that enhanced technology that provides a better customer experience because customers know exactly where their packages are along the way.
They can track things.
There's more visibility.
We have a lot of, we have a perfect, this thing called perfect.
This is a lot of information you don't need, but we have technology to ensure that the packages get where they need to go.
And that drives customer satisfaction.
So direct competitors.
When we first started, it was
Other regional carriers in the space, there was this big boom during COVID because UPS and FedEx couldn't handle the amount of volume they were getting and their performance really suffered.
And so companies like Veho started to become very successful because e-commerce companies needed a way to ship more.
So originally there were some players, I don't know, you've probably not heard of them unless you've gotten deliveries from them, but OnTrack is a big one in the space.
It was us.
There's so many, SpeedEx, all these companies.

And so companies are starting to work with, because our model is built off of like working with gig economy, obviously like Uber and DoorDash have these massive fleets of folks that can do the same thing.

They use their stores as like another warehouse arm for storage.

There's all these sort of like hybrid models that are popping up.
And
Now, those companies are also sending third-party delivery drivers to warehouses to pick up packages.
So there's a lot happening in the space, but there's a massive list of companies.
